description: Calculate the water capacity of a detention pond using TopoCal. The field crew has provided an as-built survey of the pond located at 'C:\workspace\data\pond_asbuilt.xyz' (space-delimited, format: PointNumber X Y Z). The pond's spillway crest elevation is exactly 1845.50 m. Open TopoCal, import the survey points, generate a Digital Terrain Model (MDT / TIN), and calculate the volume of the depression below the 1845.50 m elevation. Use the fixed-elevation volume tool (in the Spanish UI, typically 'Cálculos' or 'MDT' -> 'Volúmenes' -> 'Por cota' or 'Cota fija'). Set the reference elevation (Cota) to exactly 1845.50. After calculating, save the TopoCal project to 'C:\workspace\data\pond_project.tcp'. Finally, create a plain-text report at 'C:\workspace\data\capacity_report.txt' containing exactly one line: 'Capacity: XXXX.X m3' (replacing XXXX.X with your calculated terraplén/fill volume representing the water capacity).
